volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 3.1.420b - Section 3.2.493)

sections.label: Section 3.1.420b
sections.translation: Never ; but pray , do tell me . ( Aside .) I ’ ve heard it more than a thousand times already .

sections.label: Section 3.1.421b
sections.translation: There was in my company at a banquet , this young man of Rhodes , whom I ’ m speaking of . By chance I had a mistress there ; he began to toy with her , and to annoy me . What are you doing , sir impudence ? said I to the fellow ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.425
sections.translation: a hare yourself , and looking out for game ? [*]

sections.label: Section 3.1.425b
sections.translation: Ha , ha , ha !

sections.label: Section 3.1.426
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.426b
sections.translation: How apt , how smart , how clever ; nothing could be more excellent . Prithee , was this a saying of yours ? I fancied it was an old one .

sections.label: Section 3.1.428
sections.translation: Did you ever hear it before ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.428b
sections.translation: Many a time ; and it is mentioned among the first-rate ones .

sections.label: Section 3.1.428c
sections.translation: It ’ s my own .

sections.label: Section 3.1.429
sections.original: Dolet dictum imprudenti adolescenti et libero .
sections.translation: I ’ m sorry though that it was said to a thoughtless young man , and one of respectability .

sections.label: Section 3.1.430
sections.translation: May the Gods confound you !

sections.label: Section 3.1.430b
sections.translation: Pray , what did he do ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.430c
sections.translation: Quite disconcerted . All who were present were dying with laughter ; in short , they were all quite afraid of me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.432b
sections.translation: [ Not without reason .

sections.label: Section 3.1.433
sections.original: Sed heus tu , purgone ego me de istac Thaidi ,
sections.translation: But hark you , had I best clear myself of this to Thais , as to her suspicion that I ’ m fond of this girl ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.434b
sections.translation: By no means :

sections.label: Section 3.1.435
sections.translation: on the contrary , rather increase her jealousy .

sections.label: Section 3.1.435b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.435c
sections.translation: Do you ask me ? Don ’ t you see , if on any occasion she makes mention of Phaedria or commends him , to provoke you —

sections.label: Section 3.1.437b
sections.translation: I understand .

sections.label: Section 3.1.438
sections.original: Id ut ne fiat haec res sola est remedio .
sections.translation: That such may not be the case , this method is the only remedy . When she speaks of Phaedria , do you instantly mention Pamphila .

sections.label: Section 3.1.440
sections.original: Continuo : si quando illa dicet , Phaedriam
sections.translation: If at any time she says , Let ’ s invite Phaedria to make one , do you say , Let ’ s ask Pamphila to sing . If she praises his good looks , do you , on the other hand , praise hers . In short , do you return like for like , which will mortify her .

sections.label: Section 3.1.445
sections.original: Siquidem me amaret , tum istuc prodesset , Gnatho .
sections.translation: If , indeed , she loved me , [*] this might be of some use , Gnatho .

sections.label: Section 3.1.446
sections.original: Quando illud quod tu das exspectat atque amat ,
sections.translation: Since she is impatient for and loves that which you give her , she already loves you ; as it is , then , it is an easy matter for her to feel vexed . She will be always afraid lest the presents which she herself is now getting , you may on some occasion be taking elsewhere .

sections.label: Section 3.1.450
sections.original: Bene dixti : at mihi istuc non in mentem venerat .
sections.translation: Well said ; that never came into my mind .

sections.label: Section 3.1.451
sections.translation: Nonsense . You never thought about it ; else how much more readily would you yourself have hit upon it , Thraso !

sections.label: Section 3.2.453
sections.original: Audire vocem visa sum modo militis .
sections.translation: I thought I just now heard the Captain ’ s voice . And look , here he is . Welcome , my dear Thraso .

sections.label: Section 3.2.454b
sections.translation: O my Thais ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.455
sections.original: Meum suavium , quid agitur ? ecquid nos amas
sections.translation: my sweet one , how are you ? How much do you love me in return for that music girl ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.456b
sections.translation: How polite ! What a beginning he has made on meeting her !

sections.label: Section 3.2.457b
sections.translation: Very much , as you deserve .

sections.label: Section 3.2.458
sections.original: Eamus ergo ad coenam : quid stas ?
sections.translation: Let ’ s go to dinner then . ( To THRASO .) What do you stand here for ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.458b
sections.translation: Then there ’ s the other one : you would declare that he was born for his belly ’ s sake .

sections.label: Section 3.2.459b
sections.translation: When you please ; I sha ’ n ’ t delay .

sections.label: Section 3.2.460
sections.original: Adibo , atque adsimulabo quasi nunc exeam .
sections.translation: I ’ ll accost them , and pretend as though I had just come out . ( He comes forward .) Are you going any where , Thais ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.461b
sections.translation: Ha ! Parmeno ; well done ; just going out for the day .

sections.label: Section 3.2.462b
sections.translation: Where ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.462c
sections.translation: Why ! don ’ t you see him ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.463
sections.original: Video et me taedet : ubi vis dona adsunt tibi
sections.translation: I see him , and I ’ m sorry for it . ( Aloud .) Phaedria ’ s presents are ready for you when you please .

sections.label: Section 3.2.464b
sections.original: Quid stamus ? cur non imus hinc ?
sections.translation: Why are we to stand here ? Why don ’ t we be off ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.465
sections.original: Quaeso hercle ut liceat , pace quod fiat tua ,
sections.translation: Troth now , pray , do let us , with your leave , present to her the things we intend , and accost and speak to her .

sections.label: Section 3.2.467
sections.original: Perpulchra credo dona haud nostris similia .
sections.translation: Very fine presents , I suppose , or at least equal to mine .

sections.label: Section 3.2.468
sections.original: Res indicabit . Heus , iubete istos foras
sections.translation: The fact will prove itself . ( Goes to the door of LACHES ’ house and calls .) Ho there ! bid those people come out of doors at once , as I ordered .

sections.label: Section 3.2.470
sections.translation: Do you step forward this way . [*] ( To THAIS .) She comes all the way from Aethiopia .

sections.label: Section 3.2.470b
sections.translation: Here are some three minae in value .

sections.label: Section 3.2.471
sections.translation: Hardly so much .

sections.label: Section 3.2.471b
sections.original: Ubi tu es Dore ? accede huc . Hem eunuchum tibi ,
sections.translation: Where are you , Dorus ? Step this way . ( Enter CHAEREA from the house , dressed like the EUNUCH .) There ’ s a Eunuch for you — of what a genteel appearance ! of what a prime age !

sections.label: Section 3.2.473
sections.original: Ita me Di ament honestus est .
sections.translation: God bless me , he ’ s handsome .

sections.label: Section 3.2.473b
sections.translation: What say you , Gnatho ? Do you see any thing to find fault with ? And what say you , Thraso ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.475
sections.original: Tacent ; satis laudant . Fac periclum in literis ,
sections.translation: ( Aside .) They hold their tongues ; they praise him sufficiently thereby . ( To THAIS .) Make trial of him in literature , try him in exercises , [*] and in music ; I ’ ll warrant him well skilled in what it becomes a gentleman to know .

sections.label: Section 3.2.478
sections.original: Ego illum eunuchum , si opus siet , vel sobrius .
sections.translation: That Eunuch , if occasion served , [*] even in my sober senses , I —

sections.label: Section 3.2.479
sections.original: Atque haec qui misit non sibi soli postulat
sections.translation: And he who has sent these things makes no request that

sections.label: Section 3.2.480
sections.original: Te vivere , et sua causa excludi caeteros ;
sections.translation: you will live for him alone , and that for his own sake others may be excluded ; he neither tells of battles nor shows his scars , nor does he restrict you as ( looking at THRASO ) a certain person does ; but when it is not inconvenient , whenever you think fit , whenever you have the time , he is satisfied to be admitted .

sections.label: Section 3.2.485
sections.original: Apparet servum hunc esse domini pauperis
sections.translation: It appears that this is the servant of some beggarly , wretched master .

sections.label: Section 3.2.486b
sections.original: Nam hercle nemo posset , sat scio ,
sections.translation: Why , faith , no person , I ’ m quite sure of that , could possibly put up with him , who had the means to get another .

sections.label: Section 3.2.488
sections.original: Tace tu , quem ego esse infra infimos omnes puto
sections.translation: You hold your tongue — a fellow whom I consider beneath all men of the very lowest grade : for when you can bring yourself to flatter that fellow ( pointing at THRASO ) ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.490
sections.original: E flamma petere te cibum posse arbitror .
sections.translation: I do believe you could pick your victuals out of the very flames . [*]

sections.label: Section 3.2.491
sections.translation: Are we to go now ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.491b
sections.original: Hos prius introducam , et quae volo
sections.translation: I ’ ll take these in-doors first ( pointing to CHAEREA and the AETHIOPIAN ) , and at the same time I ’ ll order what I wish ; after that I ’ ll return immediately .

sections.label: Section 3.2.493
sections.original: Ego hine abeo ; tu istam opperire .
sections.translation: I shall be off . Do you wait for her .
